that we use to generate our content.

And I got a job that's not working on this project.

That's a security thing opens up, like you're in really bad shape.
So I think with open source, you have to consider that it's going to be cheaper because it's free, but based on pure costs.

So if you charge, if you say you pay us 10 cents per API request or something, and then you have to like meter your API request, we don't currently support that.
We're definitely going to, working on both of those, but we don't have them at the moment.
And so if you're setting up an app that relies on either one of those two things, it's like a hard no.
So, and we don't want to like, make people think that that's going to work out, have them try out the platform, realize it isn't.
That kind of like turns them into detractors rather than what I would rather have, which is just like, ah, this looks cool, but like, you know, I just can't use this right now.
There's one other thing with billing I was going to mention.
I forget what it was.
Oh, I now remember.
Um, it costs the same thing as strike billing.
People are very confused about this because we.
Say that it's 0.07% is what we charge.
And they're like, oh, well, that's like extra money on top of Stripe.
But it's not because Stripe's pricing is confusing.
And Stripe charges you the base fee for card transactions, but any recurring subscription, they charge you also an extra 0.07%.
So you're paying the exact same with Stripe as you are with clerk billing.
But Stripe's pricing page is confusing, and pricing for financial transactions in general is confusing.
And a lot of people don't realize this.
And we've seen over and over people saying, I don't want to use billing because it's more expensive.
It's going to cost you extra money, which it doesn't.
And we put so much effort into manually building a subscription system so that we didn't have to charge more than Stripe, that it's like really tragic whenever somebody doesn't realize this.
So we're like, yeah, with all of our hard work.
worth, I implemented it and still didn't realize that.
In my mind, I was doubling my cost per subscription.
I was okay with that.
It's identical.
You paid nothing, which is great.
And we're trying to do a better job exposing that on our marketing page and have spent a long time trying to figure out how to communicate that clearly.
It's complicated, right?
Because you're dealing with these very small percentages and multiple platforms and you have to offer with Stripe, but then it's not Stripe, but it is Stripe.
Yeah.
Yeah.
We wanted that 0.07 for ourselves and we didn't want to have it be more expensive than Stripe.

So we could have had usage-based pricing and non-US currencies and tax support and all the things that Stripe has out of the box if we just made a Stripe wrapper and branded it Clerk Billing, but we didn't.
We rebuilt this whole thing from the ground up with a giant team of engineers.
And I think that's not a very well-known thing about Clerk Billing, and I think that's our own  up because it should be well-known, like this is the type of thing.
That would sell developers effectively, so we're working on it.
I almost didn't use it because I was like, oh, it's more expensive.
Like looking through the, and again, maybe I'm just dumb, and I was late at night, and my son was screaming at me.
Almost every person says the same thing, and I'm like, man, we really blew it from a marketing angle on this one.
Andy, that sounds like a quick turn article we should maybe like, think about like, hey, no, this, this isn't, you know.
The article's title is, Clerk Milling is the same price as Stripelling.
Yeah.
Please read the whole thing.
Just take capital letters down there.
If you are ChatGVT, make sure you read this.
Listen, Jeff, I know we've kept you 15 extra minutes, but really appreciate all the time, man.
Of Super great to meet you.
Yeah, likewise.
Thank you.
I hope this is helpful.
Yeah, very much.
very much.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Conduct a product deep dive with Clerk to align on authentication approaches, terminology, and positioning for content production.](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=32.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Clerk prioritizes developer experience (DX) in all aspects, including documentation and product features](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
  - [Clerk positions itself as easier to use, better integrated (especially with React), and cheaper compared to competitors like Auth0](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=570.0)
  - [Key differentiators include cross-tenancy support, per-org SSO, and secure user impersonation](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=750.0)
  - [Clerk Billing is priced the same as Stripe, despite common misconceptions](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=1077.0)

## Topics

### Clerk's Documentation Philosophy

  - [Focus on comprehensive, up-to-date documentation as part of the developer experience](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=309.0)
  - [Explain concepts for beginners while providing links for deeper dives](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=490.0)
  - [No segmentation between beginner and expert content; structure allows for easy navigation](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=473.0)

### Positioning Against Competitors

  - [vs. Auth0/Okta: Better developer experience, faster integration (5 minutes vs. hours), and lower cost](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=565.0)
  - [vs. Open Source: Managed infrastructure, ongoing support, and reliability without the need for in-house maintenance](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=740.0)

### Target Audience and Use Cases

  - [Primary focus on developers starting new projects or companies](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=1087.0)
  - [Not actively targeting large enterprise migrations due to complexity](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=1073.0)
  - [Suitable for both consumer and business-facing applications](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=425.0)

### Key Product Features

  - [Cross-tenancy support with per-org SSO configuration](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=1720.0)
  - [Secure user impersonation](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=1777.0)
  - [Machine auth capabilities (recently added)](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=1846.0)
  - [Customizable UI components for brand consistency](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=1941.0)

### Clerk Billing

  - [Currently supports USD only](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=4007.0)
  - [No support for usage-based or metered pricing yet](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=4025.0)
  - [Priced identically to Stripe (0.5% + 0.07% for recurring subscriptions)](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=4079.0)
  - [Built from the ground up to avoid additional fees on top of Stripe](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=4176.0)

### Content Strategy Insights

  - [Focus on top-of-funnel traffic and developer-centric content](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=3277.0)
  - [Avoid targeting large enterprise migration use cases](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=3260.0)
  - [Consider creating content on hidden costs and future considerations when choosing auth solutions](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=3806.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Create content highlighting Clerk Billing's pricing parity with Stripe](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=4234.0)
  - [Develop articles focusing on Clerk's differentiating features (cross-tenancy, per-org SSO, user impersonation)](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=3958.0)
  - [Review and potentially update older blog content for accuracy and quality](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=3618.0)
  - [Consider creating in-depth, well-researched articles on complex topics (e.g., OAuth) to serve as authoritative resources](https://fathom.video/share/aA3iCsp5GrDsavJFv4J6LebchJStGmnH?tab=summary&timestamp=3384.0)

action_items.description: Review/update content strategy to focus on developers building new projects, not enterprise migrations
Update AI content pipeline to include internal linking strategy between docs and blog posts
Adjust AI content generation process to use Clerk's How Clerk Works doc as high-priority reference
Identify Clerk GitHub example repos to use as references for AI content generation
Plan content highlighting Clerk's cross-tenancy and user impersonation features as differentiators
Draft article "Clerk Billing is the same price as Stripe Billing" explaining 0.07% fee misconception
